title: User-Authentication on Wearable Devices Based on Punch Gesture Biometrics

method.input_representation: 16 statistical features including peaks, bandwidth, mean, standard deviation, duration, and initial acceleration along 3 axes
method.training_strategy: Grid search for hyperparameters nu and gamma with 10-cross validation

evaluation.context: Achieved in attacking scenario with imitation
Worst case scenario (Volunteer 3) using at most 25 training observations

evaluation_protocol: Two sessions: Session 1 tested basic authentication against other users; Session 2 tested authentication against attackers attempting to imitate the legitimate user's gesture after watching a video.

reported_limitations: Accelerometer data contains noise due to device slipping on the wrist

usability.usability_issues: Requires the user to perform a specific physical action (punch) to unlock

key_contribution: Implemented a biometric authentication system based on the unique behavioral patterns of a straight punch gesture using a smartwatch accelerometer
main_strength: High accuracy (95%+) achievable with a very small training set (approx 25 samples)
main_weakness: Requires explicit user action rather than passive monitoring
Evaluation performed on a small, homogeneous group of graduate students
insights.notes_for_review: The study specifically addresses the 'imitation' attack, which is critical for gesture-based biometrics.
